operating_state: NIKE achieved record revenues of $46.7 billion in fiscal 2022, increasing 5% reported and 6% currency-neutral, driven by NIKE Direct growth of 14% to represent 42% of total revenues with digital sales reaching $10.7 billion. Regional performance was broadly positive with double-digit growth in EMEA and APLA, North America growing moderately, while Greater China declined due to COVID-19 restrictions. Wholesale revenues declined as supply chain constraints most significantly affected wholesale channels, with footwear unit sales declining but average selling prices rising from higher NIKE Direct pricing and full-price mix improvements.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 120 basis points to 46.0% driven by NIKE Direct margin expansion, higher full-price sales mix and favorable foreign currency impacts, partially offset by elevated freight and logistics costs from supply chain disruptions and higher inventory obsolescence reserves in Greater China. Selling and administrative expenses increased due to higher strategic technology investments, wage-related expenses, NIKE Direct variable costs and normalized demand creation spending.
material_events: NIKE completed $4.0 billion in share repurchases under the four-year program and received board authorization for a new four-year $18 billion share repurchase program. The company entered definitive agreements to sell legal entities in Argentina, Uruguay and Chile to third-party distributors with closures expected by Q3 fiscal 2023. NIKE deconsolidated Russian legal entities due to economic sanctions resulting in a $96 million pre-tax charge and subsequently decided to exit the Russian marketplace entirely.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 factory shutdowns in Vietnam and Indonesia during Q1 eliminated approximately three months of production, driving inventory to $8.4 billion up 23% with extended transit times elevating in-transit inventory levels. Foreign currency translation provided favorable impact on pre-tax income through international profit translation and hedging activities, though volatility remains elevated due to geopolitical dynamics and stronger U.S. Dollar.
forward_outlook: Management expects inventory supply to significantly lag consumer demand with transportation and logistics costs remaining elevated while product costs stay high due to input cost inflation. Annual capital expenditures of approximately 3% of revenues will focus on digital capabilities, technology foundation and supply chain improvements. The company maintains multi-year investment plans for digital transformation and brand campaigns while continuing Consumer Direct Acceleration strategy execution.
uncertainty_profile: Operating environment volatility expected to continue with ongoing COVID-19 variant risks potentially disrupting operations and impacting revenue growth and profitability. Argentina subsidiary operates in hyperinflationary environment requiring U.S. Dollar functional currency with outstanding endorsement and product purchase obligations representing significant future cash commitments. Cybersecurity and climate change risks have increased requiring additional facility investments affecting raw material availability and costs.
